relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Law rules only within the span of the old life-order.
Marriage law illustrates release through death.
Death changes the binding and opens new belonging.
Through Messiah’s body, old binding dies so fruit can be borne to the Divine.
In flesh-patterns, misaligned passions bore fruit for death.
Now service moves in newness of Spirit, not oldness of letter.
Law is not misalignment; Law reveals misalignment.
Misalignment uses commandment as opportunity to awaken desire.
Commandment arrival exposes sin’s life and the self’s death.
The commandment intended for life becomes death through misuse by sin.
Misalignment deceives and kills through the commandment.
Law and commandment remain holy, just, and good.
The good exposes sin as exceedingly misaligned.
Law is spiritual; the fleshly self is sold under sin’s power.
The divided self does what it does not understand or will.
Hating the act confirms the Law’s goodness.
Sin dwelling within acts like an occupying power.
Willing the good is present; accomplishing it is fractured.
The unwanted harmful thing keeps being practiced.
The split reveals indwelling misalignment, not the deepest self.
A principle appears: the harmful is present when good is willed.
The inner human delights in Divine Law.
Another principle wars in the members and captures the mind.
The cry rises: who will rescue me from this death-body?
Thanks comes through Jesus Messiah; the split is named before Romans 8 opens the Spirit-way.

contemplative_note: Romans 7 names the inner war without making the body evil or Torah evil. The problem is indwelling misalignment as a parasitic power; the rescue opens in Spirit.
